thejournal_ie-2022.text:
Weekly Podcast
            The Explainer
            The Explainer is a weekly podcast from TheJournal.ie that takes a deeper look at one big news story you need to know about. Listen now wherever you get your podcasts

The42
                    
                    
                        The latest Irish and international sports news for readers and members

Noteworthy
                    
                    
                        A platform helping fund the type of in-depth journalism that the public wants to see

You can now get an instant online valuation for your home free on Daft - here's how it works
                
                    The new tool from Daft gives property owners an initial indication of what their property might be worth.

Evening Wrap - 12 hours ago            
            The 5 at 5
            Five minutes, five stories, five o’clock…
Man jailed for four and a half months after assaulting his partner in 2020
State is allowing calves to be transported to Europe in a cruel manner, claims NGO
Unprecedented peak demand for water in the UK
Man (50s) dies following a house fire in his home in Drumcondra
Keir Starmer denies using Holocaust memorial for political purposes
'I was really close to signing for Leinster. They gave my contract to James Lowe!'

Money Diaries: An administration assistant on €31K living in Co Clare
This week, our reader enjoys cooking at home and cycling to keep fit while saving for a long-term plan of investing in a property.
Opinion: Is the Ukraine invasion finally whipping back the curtain to reveal the backstage grime of society?
Author Sam O’Brien explores what the current geopolitical situation tells us about what lies behind echelons of power.
Extract: I was in the prison when the call came through: 'We have a situation'
Former prison officer David McDonald has written a book about his career – in this extract, he writes about the beginning of a riot.
Opinion: As Boris departs, the British public should ask the Conservatives 'what took you so long?'
Cabinet members and Conservative backbenchers stuck with Johnson while the public cried for him to leave, former UK/EU negotiatior Sydney Nash writes.
Money Diaries: A HR assistant on €27K living in the northeast of the country
This week, our reader feels lucky to be saving on rent while living at home, but feels like she is struggling to make big moves for her future.
